question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot environment variable override mechanism by setting `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_RECORD_FREQUENCY=PT10S` in a shared container environment (e.g., via a compromised sidecar container), causing the record downloader's polling interval to increase to 10 seconds (500%+ of the ~2s average block time) and freezing the mirror node's transaction view?
